violations.text: The presence of flies are not controlled as evidenced by live activity. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Inspector observed a live cat in basement/food storage area. PIC informed to remove live animal from facility. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025
Inspector observed standing liquid waste in sub pump basin. PIC informed to remove liquid waste and keep covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
The dumpster was observed with an overflow of trash due to lack of pickup. PIC stated pickup has been scheduled. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2025
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
The hand-washing sink in the meat cutting area and bathroom were lacking paper towels. PIC restocked. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the meat cutting area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. PIC restocked. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
The hand-washing sink in the bathroom was lacking proper hand-washing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

Cheese in a zip-lock bag and yogurt beverage were observed from an unapproved source and the labels did not contain required information. PIC disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
A package of hot dogs was observed with discoloration and a package of smoked meat was observed with slime and discoloration. PIC disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs and chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as carrots and salami) in the walk-in and open case refrigerators. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The following food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. - knives, - slicers, - meat grinder, - ban saw, - cutting boards. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

Various items were observed held between 46-56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded items and service arrived during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

Cheeses and deli meat were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Inspector observed fly traps above preparation areas in meat cutting area. PIC informed to remove. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Inspector observed dead flies on multiple traps throughout the establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The presence of flies are not controlled as evidenced by small live flies. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the front and side doors, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025
Food items were observed stored on the floor in the retail area and walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Single use items were observed stored around hand-washing sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Walk-in unit located in the basement area (leak into large trashcan) and meat display unit (cracked door) were not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris:- meat display cases,- walk-in cooler fans,- retail cooler shelves. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Leak observed in pipe in the basement. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
Grease trap was observed in poor repair with puddle of raw sewage surrounding device in the basement area. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
The receptacle in the toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
Inspector observed a heavy accumulation of trash/boxes and other debris in the refuse area. Area to be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The walk-in cooler in the basement area (broken FRP board with exposed wood) and ceiling tiles in the retail and meat cutting areas are in poor repair previous water damage). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

Food contact surfaces of the slicer was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023

Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in units, on wood pallets in market and in basement, and stored in milk crates. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023

Wood is used in the construction of shelving in the basement prep area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023

walk-n units located in the basement area were not in good repair. Leak from ceiling and pipes was observed in the walk-in refrigerator. Leaks and an accumulation of ice were observed on the exterior condensate lines of the walk-in freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023
The framing around the prep area in the basement area is unfinished, observed with exposed wood and wallboard. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items and pooling of water were observed in the basement. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
